strengths: Long full-stack tenure with TypeScript, Node.js, React and MongoDB across multiple Berlin-based product companies
Founder-level ownership of GISCollective shows architecture, Kubernetes deployment and end-to-end delivery experience
Recent re:cinq role explicitly includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, matching the JD's AI agent requirement
concerns: No demonstrated Nest.js experience and Vitest is not mentioned anywhere in the resume, despite both being part of the primary stack
Kotlin is entirely absent and Java exposure is limited to older robotics/LabWindows work, leaving the secondary stack requirement thinly covered

Currently on a career gap since October 2025 with a heavy historical lean on DLang — a niche language unrelated to the JD — raising questions about focus and current hands-on momentum in the required stack

key_factors.factor: Weak Kotlin/Java coverage for secondary stack
Significant DLang focus and active career gap

response.justification: The candidate's resume shows broad full-stack tenure but lacks explicit evidence of two pillars of the required primary stack — Nest.js and Vitest — and offers only dated, narrow Java exposure with no Kotlin at all, leaving the secondary stack effectively uncovered. A substantial portion of recent and self-directed work centers on DLang, a niche language irrelevant to this role, while the current career gap since October 2025 further weakens signals of momentum in the exact TypeScript/Nest.js/React ecosystem the team needs. On balance, the fit to the specific stack and seniority expectations is weak.
